revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = | width = | caption = | type = Bekkaku Kanpeisha,別格官幣社 | dedication = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]],[[Kikuchi Takeshige]],[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded = April 28, 1870 | closed = | founder = [[Emperor Meiji]] | priest = | address = 1257, Waifu,[[ Kikuchi]]-shi,[[ Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | phone = | website =http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' is a [[shrine]], in [[Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]](菊池武時,(1292-Apr 27, 1333),[[Kikuchi Takeshige]](菊池武重,（1307? - 1338?), and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]](菊池武光,1319?-1373)] are enshrined. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built in the Kikuchi castle, [[Kikuchi]][[Kumamoto Prefecture]]. [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was enshrined on April 28,1870. The rank was Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923,[[Kikuchi Takeshige]] and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] are enshrined, in addition. On September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were built. On April 28, 2000,the 130th year aniversary of the shrine was performed. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful daimyo family of [[Higo]],[[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]]. They were the forerunners of [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333-1336).In 1333,[[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him. He was Go-Daigo's first man and was awarded for this. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack Chinzei Tandai's Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in this attack but they were the forerunners of [[Kenmu Restoration]](133-1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long banboo trees, a new weapon. He strengthened the tie of the [[Kikuchi clan]] with a Kikuchi const itution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu]]. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine] [[ja:菊池神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shrine of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kumamoto]] [[Category:Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}}

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = | width = | caption = | type = Bekkaku Kanpeisha,別格官幣社 | dedication = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]],Kikuchi Takeshige,[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded = April 28, 1870 | closed = | founder = [[Emperor Meiji]] | priest = | address = 1257, Waifu,[[ Kikuchi]]-shi,[[ Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | phone = | website =http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' is a [[shrine]], in [[Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]](菊池武時,(1292-Apr 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige(菊池武重,（1307? - 1338?), and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]](菊池武光,1319?-1373) are enshrined. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built in the Kikuchi castle, [[Kikuchi]][[Kumamoto Prefecture]]. [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was enshrined on April 28,1870. The rank was Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] are enshrined, in addition. On September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were built. On April 28, 2000,the 130th year aniversary of the shrine was performed. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful daimyo family of [[Higo]],[[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]]. In 1333,[[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him. He was Go-Daigo's first man and was awarded for this. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack Chinzei Tandai's Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in this attack but they were the forerunners of [[Kenmu Restoration]](1333-1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long banboo trees, a new weapon. He strengthened the tie of the [[Kikuchi clan]] with a Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu]]. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine] [[ja:菊池神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shrine of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kumamoto]] [[Category:Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}}

In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were built. On April 28, 2000,the 130th year anniversary of the shrine was performed. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful daimyo family of [[Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]]. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him. He was Go-Daigo's first man and was awarded for this. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack Chinzei Tandai's Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in this attack but they were the forerunners of [[Kenmu Restoration]](1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long banboo trees, a new weapon. He strengthened the tie of the [[Kikuchi clan]] with a Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu]]. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shrine of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumamoto]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:菊池神社]]

In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were built. On April 28, 2000, the 130th year anniversary of the shrine was performed. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful daimyo family of [[Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]]. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him. He was Go-Daigo's first man and was awarded for this. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack Chinzei Tandai's Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in this attack but they were the forerunners of [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long banboo trees, a new weapon. He strengthened the tie of the [[Kikuchi clan]] with a Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu]]. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://wgordon.web.wesleyan.edu/kamikaze/monuments/matsuo-kikuchi/index.htm Keiu Matsuo Monument at Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shrine of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumamoto]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]]

Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The rank was Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] are enshrined, in addition. On September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were built. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him. He was Go-Daigo's first man and was awarded for this. In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] are enshrined, in addition. On September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were built. On April 28, 2000, the 130th year anniversary of the shrine was performed. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful [[daimyo]] family of [[Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him. He was Go-Daigo's first man and was awarded for this. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack Chinzei Tandai's Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in this attack but they were the forerunners of [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo trees, a new weapon. He strengthened the tie of the Kikuchi clan with a Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikuchi Yoshiyuki 2.jpg | width = | caption = Portrait said to be of [[Kikuchi Yoshiyuki]] | type = Bekkaku Kanpeisha,別格官幣社 | dedication = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded = April 28, 1870 | closed = | founder = [[Emperor Meiji]] | priest = | address = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | phone = | website = http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292-Apr 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, 1319?-1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. On September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th year anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful [[daimyo]] family of [[Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}}

He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]]

Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia1]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia01]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]]

It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia18]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia18]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia01]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]]

It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia18]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia18]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia04]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} [[category:Pages that used He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links==
