On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kikuchi Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. It is one of the [[Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:c]][[Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]]

Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[Kikuchi Taketoki]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[Kikuchi Taketoki]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[Kikuchi Taketoki]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|59|16|N|130|48|58|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], Kikuchi Takeshige, [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] [[Category:Expanded Pages from Wikipedia]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine

It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}}

He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q94317]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q94317]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = File:Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.98777778|N|130.81611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Kikuchi|WD=Q911709}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

菊池神社 | image = File:Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.98777778|N|130.81611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Kikuchi|WD=Q911709}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q94317]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q94317]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = File:Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.98777778|N|130.81611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Kikuchi|WD=Q911709}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

菊池神社 | image = File:Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.98777778|N|130.81611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi-shi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Kikuchi|WD=Q911709}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Kikuchi, Kumamoto|Kikuchi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], in which [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *[[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and [[:en:Kikuchi Takemitsu|Kikuchi Takemitsu]] were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]] was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo]], [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *[[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]] was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the [[:en:Chinzei Bugyō|Defense Commissioner of the West]] Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]] city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]], and the eldest son of [[:en:Kikuchi clan|Kikuchi clan]]. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]]. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *[[:en:History of Kumamoto Prefecture|History of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q94317]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q94317]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine

Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Defense Commissioner of the West}} Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}} city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}}, and the eldest son of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}}. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *{{ill|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|en|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q11568214|lt=History of Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

菊池神社 | image = File:Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.98777778|N|130.81611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, {{ill|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|en|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|WD=Q911709|lt=Kikuchi-shi}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Kikuchi|WD=Q911709}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|en|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|WD=Q911709|lt=Kikuchi}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, in which {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}} (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and {{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|WD=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *{{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|WD=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}} where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}}, who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and {{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|WD=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}} (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}} was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo}}, [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *{{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}} was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}} asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Defense Commissioner of the West}} Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}} city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}}, and the eldest son of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}}. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *{{ill|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|en|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q11568214|lt=History of Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

菊池神社 | image = File:Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.98777778|N|130.81611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, {{ill|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|en|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|WD=Q911709|lt=Kikuchi-shi}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Kikuchi|WD=Q911709}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|en|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|WD=Q911709|lt=Kikuchi}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, in which {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}} (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and {{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|WD=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *{{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|WD=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}} where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}}, who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and {{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|WD=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}} (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}} was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo}}, [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *{{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}} was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}} asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Defense Commissioner of the West}} Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}} city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}}, and the eldest son of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan}}. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *{{ill|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|en|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q11568214|lt=History of Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}} (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province|1=Higo Province}} was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo|1=Higo Province}}, [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *{{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}} was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Defense Commissioner of the West|1=Chinzei Bugyō}} Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}}, and the eldest son of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}}. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *{{ill|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|en|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q11568214|lt=History of Kumamoto Prefecture|1=History of Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
