[[co:commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine]][[en:Kikuchi Shrine]][[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]][[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = File:Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.98777778|N|130.81611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316|1=Kikuchi Taketoki}} | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, {{ill|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|en|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|WD=Q911709|lt=Kikuchi-shi|1=Kikuchi, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Kikuchi|WD=Q911709|1=Kikuchi, Kumamoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|en|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|WD=Q911709|lt=Kikuchi|1=Kikuchi, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, in which {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}} (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and {{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|WD=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu|1=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *{{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|WD=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu|1=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}}, who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and {{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|WD=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu|1=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}} (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province|1=Higo Province}} was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo|1=Higo Province}}, [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *{{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}} was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|WD=Q440978|lt=Defense Commissioner of the West|1=Chinzei Bugyō}} Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}}, and the eldest son of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|WD=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}}. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu, Fukuoka}}. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *{{ill|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|en|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q11568214|lt=History of Kumamoto Prefecture|1=History of Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ [[en:Kikuchi Shrine]] [[fr:Kikuchi-jinja]] [[ja:菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{wikidata link|Q94317}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine
菊池神社 | image = File:Kikushi-shrine.jpg | image_upright = 1.2 | caption = Kikuchi Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.98777778|N|130.81611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]],別格官幣社 | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|qid=Q94316|1=Kikuchi Taketoki}} | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} | established = April 28, 1870 | date_destroyed = | location = 1257, Waifu, {{ill|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|en|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|qid=Q911709|lt=Kikuchi-shi|1=Kikuchi, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, Japan 861-1331 | website = {{URL|http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Kikuchi|qid=Q911709|1=Kikuchi, Kumamoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kikuchi Shrine'''|菊池神社|Kikuchi Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|en|Kikuchi, Kumamoto|qid=Q911709|lt=Kikuchi|1=Kikuchi, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, in which {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|qid=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}} (菊池武時, 1292 – April 27, 1333), Kikuchi Takeshige (菊池武重, 1307?-1338?) and {{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|qid=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu|1=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} (菊池武光, {{circa|1319}} – 1373) are enshrined. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==Another Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)== *{{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|qid=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu|1=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} is also enshrined in a Kikuchi shrine at 7-10-1, Nanakuma, Johnan-ku, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|qid=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} where he died at war. ==History of the shrine== *With the suggestion of Nagaoka Masami, {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} ordered in 1868, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|qid=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}}, who worked for the emperors, and a shrine was built on the site of the old Kikuchi castle in Kikuchi City. Kikuchi Taketoki was enshrined on April 28, 1870. The shrine is classified as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha (shrines for those with distinguished services to the state). In March 1923, Kikuchi Takeshige and {{ill|Kikuchi Takemitsu|en|Kikuchi Takemitsu|qid=Q2379919|lt=Kikuchi Takemitsu|1=Kikuchi Takemitsu}} were also enshrined at the same location. In September 1952, the shrine was made a [[Religious corporation]]. In 1970, a Kumonoe-guu building and a historical museum were also built on shrine grounds. On April 28, 2000, the shrine celebrated its 130th anniversary. ==Kikuchi clan and Kikuchi Taketoki== *The {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|qid=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}} (菊池氏, Kikuchi-shi) of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|qid=Q259705|lt=Higo Province|1=Higo Province}} was a powerful ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' family of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|qid=Q259705|lt=Higo|1=Higo Province}}, [[Kyushu]]. The Kikuchi lineage was renowned for its valiant service in defense of the emperor and against foreign invaders. *{{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|qid=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}} was the 11th head of the Kikuchi clan. In 1333, {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} asked Taketoki to help him overthrow the shogunate and restore imperial rule. He was Go-Daigo's right-hand man and was awarded greatly for his loyalty. Taketoki gathered many people in Kyushu and was planning to attack the {{ill|Chinzei Bugyō|en|Chinzei Bugyō|qid=Q440978|lt=Defense Commissioner of the West|1=Chinzei Bugyō}} Hojo Hidetoki (Akahashi Hidetoki) but their plan was leaked. Taketoki and his son Yoritaka died in the attack, but they were the forerunners of the [[Kenmu Restoration]] (1333–1336). Taketoki was buried in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|qid=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} city where he died. ==Kikuchi Takeshige== *The 12th head of the {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|qid=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}}, and the eldest son of {{ill|Kikuchi clan|en|Kikuchi clan|qid=Q2975292|lt=Kikuchi clan|1=Kikuchi clan}}. He invented the Kikuchi 1000 spears or shortened spears on long bamboo poles, a new weapon. He strengthened the bond of the Kikuchi clan by introducing the Kikuchi constitution. ==Kikuchi Takemitsu== *He finally unified [[Kyushu]] by attacking {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|qid=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu, Fukuoka}}. He was a staunch supporter of the Southern Court set up by Emperor Go-Daigo after the Kenmu Restoration failed. ==References== *Pamphlet of Kikuchi Shrine on November 21, 2010. ==External links== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/higo/kikuti_title.htm Kikuchi Shrine菊池神社]（玄松子） *[http://nobyama.com/kikuchi.html Kikuchi Shrine] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20110530150143/http://www.mapple.net/spots/G04300034601.htm Kikuchi Shrine] ==See also== *{{ill|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|en|History of Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q11568214|lt=History of Kumamoto Prefecture|1=History of Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} \ {{wikidata link|Q94317|co|commons:Category:Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|fr|Kikuchi-jinja|ja|菊池神社 (菊池市)}} [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
